title: status-pipe ticket state file
description: One file per tracked ticket at .status-pipe/tickets/<key>.json. Owned by the agent side (orchestrator/worker); the extension only reads it.

repo.description: owner/name, cross-checked against the git remote

ticket.description: Opaque ticket key: GitHub issue number as a string ('853') or Jira key ('PROJ-123'). Must equal the filename stem.

headline.description: One present-tense sentence: what just happened, operator-readable. For every optional field, an explicit null is equivalent to omitting it (the readers are tolerant; writers may emit either).

ref.description: Deep link to the exact comment/run/PR being waited on

part.description: Free-form part label within the ticket's stack, e.g. 'T1a'

blockers.description: Non-empty forces the NEEDS YOU lane regardless of health

plan.description: The worker's current plan for this ticket, carried across passes. Rewritten as the plan evolves (NOT append-only). Lets a fresh pass resume from a known plan instead of re-deriving one. Agent-owned working memory.

deadEnds.description: Append-only record of approaches that were tried and failed, so a later pass does not repeat them. Agent-owned working memory.

doNotRetryWithout.description: What would have to change before this is worth retrying (e.g. 'operator runs the CI snapshot job'). null = a hard dead-end.

notes.description: Free-form scratchpad: the worker's mental model of the code/task worth carrying across passes but not fitting plan/deadEnds. Agent-owned; not rendered by the extension.

subTickets.description: Discussion channels carved out of an epic's tracking ticket. Not work items.

agentCommentIds.description: Forge API ids of comments posted by the agent itself (written by plugin/bin/post-comment). Trust input for shared-account self-recognition; never rendered.

history.description: Append-only log; ack consumption/supersession is recorded here naming the ackId.
